Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf To Submit Her Claims To Individual Arbitration;
Off Calendar Nov-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT LEGACY PARTNERS RESIDENTIAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT HER CLAIMS TO INDIVIDUAL ARBITRATION.

APPEARANCES: Alexander Wheeler, Esq. 661/949-2595 for pltf & opposing party; Shawn M. Larsen, Esq. of Rutan & Tucker, LLP 714/641-5100 for deft & moving party (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted. The court finds a valid arbitration agreement and the dispute is covered by the arbitration agreement. Plaintiff fails to establish a ground for denial of the motion. The agreement is not procedurally or substantively unconscionable. the agreement signed by plaintiff clearly addresses binding arbitration. The agreement is silent regarding class actions, and plaintiff does not demonstrate that the case falls within the rules established by Gentry v. Superior Court (2007) 42 CAL 4th 443, and Discover Bank v. Superior Court (2005) 36 CAL 4th 148. Strong public policy favors arbitration. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
